page: Amazon Web Services i3.2xlarge is a storage-optimized virtual server instance running on the Xen hypervisor. It features 8 vCPUs and 4 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor operating at 2.3 GHz. The instance is equipped with 61.0 GB of system memory, providing 7.62 GB per core. A key hardware feature is the inclusion of 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, designed for high-throughput and low-latency data access. It does not include any GPU hardware. Baseline network bandwidth is rated at 2 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate that the CPU performance is relatively modest. The single-core stress-ng performance of 883.49 and multi-core score of 4158.51 fall into the bottom 10% and bottom 25% categories respectively. Similarly, Geekbench single-core (893.0) and multi-core (3348.0) scores reflect weak to poor processing capabilities compared to newer hardware generations. Memory bandwidth tests show average performance for large uncached blocks at 41.72 GB/sec, while small cached block reads are weaker at 315.49 GB/sec. Database operations on Passmark scored 2417.31, and Redis SET operations achieved 2.37 million operations per second, which is in the bottom 10%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the i3.2xlarge offers a high density of local NVMe SSD storage and system memory relative to its compute resources. However, the tradeoff is its older Intel Xeon architecture, which delivers lower compute performance per vCPU. This makes the instance less suitable for CPU-heavy workloads, but highly efficient for storage-bound applications where local disk capacity and memory size are the primary bottlenecks.

Given its hardware profile, the i3.2xlarge is best suited for storage-intensive workloads, local database hosting, NoSQL databases, and transactional systems that require large local scratch space or caching layers. It is less appropriate for intensive computational tasks, software compilation, or large-scale LLM inference.
description: Amazon Web Services i3.2xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs and 4 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz. It is equipped with 61.0 GB of memory and a bundled 1900 GB local NVMe SSD, operating on the Xen hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. While its CPU performance is modest—with Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores falling into the lower percentiles—it offers a high density of local storage and memory. This makes the instance highly cost-efficient for storage-bound workloads rather than compute-heavy tasks. The i3.2xlarge is best suited for local databases, NoSQL systems, and transactional applications requiring substantial local NVMe storage and moderate memory capacity.

tagline: A storage-optimized virtual server featuring high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage and balanced memory for data-intensive workloads.

61.0 GB of system memory providing 7.62 GB per core
1900 GB of bundled local NVMe SSD storage

Best suited for local databases, NoSQL platforms, and transactional storage workloads
